Your doctor orders labs. You get the results. Maybe you glance at the red and green flags. Then they go in a drawer.
But tracking lab results over time is where the real insight lives. One cholesterol reading means little. A year of readings shows whether things are heading the right direction.
We wrote about why this matters and how to actually do it.
docs.trackmeplus.com/2026/03/31/why-tracking-lab-results-matters

Managing medications for a parent or loved one is one of those jobs that sounds simple until you're doing it. Multiple prescriptions, different dosing schedules, refill dates, and lab results to track between doctor visits.
TrackMe+ Family Plan lets you set up a dedicated account for your loved one. Track their meds, labs, and health data. Up to 4 accounts, each fully featured.

If you're tracking health for more than one person, you know the pain. Dad's blood pressure meds, Mom's supplements, your own prescriptions. Different schedules, different pharmacies, different everything.
TrackMe+ Family Plan covers up to 4 people. Each person gets their own account with full medication, lab, and health tracking. Set one up for a loved one, or let them use it independently.

"Mom takes 7 medications. I was using sticky notes and a spreadsheet." We hear this a lot.
The sticky note system works until it doesn't. A medication changes, a refill date shifts, and suddenly the whole system falls apart. TrackMe+ replaces that fragile setup with one app that tracks doses, refill dates, lab results, and supply levels. Set it up once and the app keeps everything current.

Anthropic just raised $30 billion at a $900 billion valuation. That makes it the most valuable private AI company in the

OpenAI just launched a new company called DeployCo, backed by $4 billion. Their product is what they call "Forward Deployed Engineers." Actual humans placed inside client businesses to implement AI. The thesis is simple: AI does not work well as a self-serve product. It needs someone embedded in your operations who understands both the tools and the business. NexHam has been operating on exactly that model. We do not sell you software and walk away. We get into your workflows, understand your specific problems, and build something that actually fits. The difference is we do not cost $4 billion to get started. nexhamcorp.com

Every week there is another AI announcement. Another tool, another valuation, another reason to feel behind. Here is what actually helps: pick one problem your business has right now. Something that is costing you 5 or more hours a week. Research, follow-up emails, scheduling, proposals. Pick one. Then solve that with AI. Just that one thing. Once it works, you will know what to do next. The owners who are getting the most out of AI are not the ones with the most tools. They are the ones who solved one problem well and then moved to the next. Start there. nexhamcorp.com if you want help picking the right starting point.

The AI tools NexHam uses just shipped 33 updates in a single release. That was Tuesday. The week before had its own batch. And next week will too. This is the pace now. Not for a year. Not until things settle. This is just what working with AI tools looks like. The owners who are going to win with this are not the ones trying to keep up with every release. They are the ones who stopped chasing updates and started focusing on what actually works for their business right now. The tools will keep getting better. What matters is whether you have a process that can absorb that without constant disruption. That is what NexHam helps build.

There is a conversation most business owners skip: which parts of my job do I actually want AI handling? The answer is not "everything." And it is not "nothing." It is something specific. AI is excellent at repeatable tasks: drafting, summarizing, organizing, scheduling, follow-up sequencing. It handles volume fast and does not get tired. What it does not replace: your read on a client, your judgment on pricing, your decision to let someone go or bring someone on. The owners who get frustrated with AI usually ran it at problems that needed judgment. The ones who get results started with the repeatable stuff. Figure out which category your biggest time-drains fall into. Start there.

AI news moved fast this week. A $30B raise. A new $4B deployment company. A major platform update. It is a lot to make sense of when you are also trying to run a business. NexHam's job is not to explain all of it. It is to look at your specific situation and identify the two or three things that actually matter for your business right now. If you want that conversation, nexhamcorp.com is where to start.
